title: Parliament Monsoon Session Day 4 LIVE: Both Houses adjourned till noon; Govt. ready for NEET discussion today, but Congress must not put conditions, says Rijiju
content: LoP in Rajya Sabha Mallikarjun Kharge reiterated the Opposition's demand of Union Education Minister Dharmendra Pradhan's resignation

title: Jantar Mantar protest: Annamalai urges BJP government to engage in meaningful dialogue with students
content: “If our students lose faith in the fairness and credibility of our education system, we are jeopardising the future of our nation,” the former BJP leader said

title: 3,500 VBA workers detained ahead of Maharashtra bandh called by us: Prakash Ambedkar
content: VBA party chief Mr. Ambedkar appealed for a Statewide bandh to be observed in protest of the Centre’s education policies and the alleged police action against students and agitators in Delhi

title: Protesters at Jantar Mantar find relief at free clinics set up by doctors
content: The clinic, apart from having paracetamol, painkillers, bottles of Dettol, and sanitary pads, also has a machine to check blood pressure, an oximeter, and thermometers. The medicines and the equipment were donated by the people and the 10-12 doctors working there

title: VACB’s ‘Operation Parpidam’ exposes widespread municipal corruption in Kerala’s construction sector
content: Statewide inspections also point to how local networks of corrupt officials and their agents have significantly escalated the cost of construction for ordinary families

title: Jana Nayagan gets ‘A’ certificate; likely to release in theatres on July 23
content: According to reports, after the makers implemented modifications suggested by the examining committee, the film was initially considered suitable for a 'U/A' certificate; however, the process stalled when a committee member raised an internal objection, claiming certain portions could hurt public sentiments.

title: Vijayapura MLA Basanagouda Patil Yatnal seeks action against fake news
content: The MLA distributed awards to senior journalists and felicitated meritorious children of journalists. The award ceremony was held at the Kandgal Hanumantharaya Ranga Mandir

title: India's Ambassador Kwatra meets U.S. Senator Sheehy, discusses defence ties
content: India’s envoy to the U.S. Vinay Mohan Kwatra discussed the vital defence partnership between the two nations and appreciated U.S. Senator Tim Sheehy’s support for the India-U.S. partnership

title: Allahabad HC split on ‘bulldozer justice’: So what divided the Bench | Explained
content: Two judges differed on how far the High Court can go in creating additional safeguards beyond those already laid down by the Supreme Court

title: Government to set up fast-track courts for paper leak cases, says PM Modi
content: “Nothing is more important than the welfare and future of our youth!” said Prime Minister Narendra Modi

title: Jantar Mantar protest LIVE: CJP demands Dharmendra Pradhan’s resignation in response to PM Modi’s assurance of probe into paper leaks
content: Delhi Metro shuts 16 stations, including Rajiv Chowk, Patel Chowk, Delhi Gate and Central Secretariat; interchange facility available at Rajiv Chowk, Mandi House and Central Secretariat

title: No deliverance: when gig workers fall prey to road accidents
content: As the number of workers employed by delivery platforms increases, data from various States, collected by trade unions, show that accidents, injuries, and deaths among them have been on the rise over the years. Though the Union government maintains that gig workers will be covered under the Code of Social Security, details made available in Parliament show that many families of the workers who died or suffered injuries during work did not receive the accidental or death insurance benefits under the Pradhan Mantri Suraksha Bima Yojana (PMSBY)

title: Quality research and ethical publications key to academic success, says CUK Vice-Chancellor
content: CUK V-C Prof. Battu Sathyanarayana urged research scholars to choose meaningful research topics, avoid plagiarism, deposit their theses in Shodhganga, publish in reputed journals, and uphold the highest standards of academic integrity

title: Supreme Court metro station closure affecting lawyers, says Bar Association president
content: Chief Justice of India (CJI) Surya Kant informed the Bar that he had passed an administrative order directing that no adverse orders be passed solely on account of the non-appearance of counsel

title: Tobacco farmers meet Jagan Mohan Reddy, seek faster procurement; YSRCP promises to press government
content: Though 94 days have passed since the commencement of tobacco auctions, procurement is far from complete, leaving thousands of growers in severe financial distress, they say

title: Bengaluru Tech Summit ropes in European partners to create global growth opportunities for startups
content: The collaborations are aimed at providing start-ups in Karnataka access to international markets, investors, innovation networks and cross-border partnerships

title: Centre positive on cutting GST on tobacco exports from 40% to 28%: Yashwanth Kumar
content: The Union Government has assured the A.P. delegation that alternative incentive mechanisms will be explored to safeguard farmers’ interests

title: Rift in AMMA triggers fresh row over ‘power group’ in Malayalam cinema
content: Allegation by Shwetha Menon that the ‘power group’ named in Hema Committee report is working against her has triggered a fresh bout of infighting within AMMA even as her rival camp has asked her to divulge the names of those named in the controversial report

title: Sikkim tunnel disaster: 22 bodies recovered; search and rescue ops continue for remaining victims
content: Officials said personnel of multiple agencies, including those specialising in coal-bearing areas, conducted operations until midnight on Wednesday under difficult condition

title: Sabarimala gold theft case: SIT detains former TDB president P.S. Prasanth for questioning
content: Prasanth’s detention comes nearly three weeks after SIT questioned Unnikrishnan Potti, the prime accused in the case. The move comes hours after Prasanth posted on Facebook that his arrest in the case was imminent

title: Realtors filed defective PIL on Bidadi township project to derail our movement: Farmers
content: A petition, which was dismissed by the HC, was not only ‘defective’ but also a deliberate attempt by ‘vested interests’ to prevent farmers from pursuing their genuine legal battle against land acquisition

title: Bengaluru voices at Freedom Park: ‘Why isn’t Dharmendra Pradhan saying anything?’
content: Scores of students gathered at Freedom Park in Central Bengaluru condemning the police lathi charge on students who were marching to Parliament protesting against NEET irregularities

title: BDA puts checks on builders seeking plan modifications in Bengaluru
content: The BDA has made it clear that no modified building plan can be sanctioned once the permissible Floor Area Ratio has been fully utilised and an Occupancy Certificate issued for the entire project

title: Gujarat approves de-addiction centres in all district hospitals
content: According to the government, each centre will provide scientific detoxification, de-addiction therapy, mental healthcare, family counselling and rehabilitation services

title: Andhra Pradesh registers six new COVID cases, three each from East Godavari, Vizag districts
content: The total case load in the State rises to 32, while the death toll stands at four, says Health Department

title: Amid power crisis, workplace accidents pose stiff challenge to Kerala State Electricity Board
content: Accidents have claimed the lives of 8 KSEB staff and 10 contract staff so far this year, notes KSEB. Primary causes include safety rule violations and carelessness.

title: Quality sleep key to protecting brain health amid rising screen time, say neurologists
content: Doctors say sleep is not merely a period of rest but a critical biological process during which the brain repairs itself, consolidates memories and clears metabolic waste accumulated during waking hours

title: 50 OTT platforms disabled over the last two years | Explained
content: What is the legal framework governing OTT platforms? What are the other related laws? Why is the action significant? What is the way forward?

title: NCB dismantles transnational drug syndicate, arrests Myanmar-based “kingpin”
content: The accused is wanted in three cases registered by the NCB and is also connected to seven other cases instituted by the police and the DRI

title: Africans, Russians, Ukrainians among 2,089 deported from Assam since 2016
content: The State government tells the Assam Assembly that 61,259 Hindus and Christians were among 1,72,673 foreigners detected in Assam till May 31

title: 19-year-old may lose vision in right eye due to pellet injury, says family
content: Sahil Lochab underwent a surgery to remove pellets; doctors have informed that there is only a “1% chance” of vision returning in the injured eye as the pupil was damaged, his family said

title: 10 more deaths raise Assam flood toll to 41
content: Chief Minister Himanta Biswa Sarma says the damage is far greater than anticipated and more bodies are being recovered from the affected districts; more rains forecast over next four days

title: PM should apologise to students, Education Minister must resign: Rahul Gandhi
content: The Congress leader says there have been 156 paper leaks and ‘zero convictions’, the Opposition is ‘100% in agreement’ with the protesters’ demands, describing them as ‘legitimate’ and ‘non-negotiable’

title: Pellet guns, shock batons used against protesters in Delhi; police deny
content: Shaikh Irshad Mansoori suffered pellet injuries, the first known case of security forces using pellet guns in Delhi; police deny use, say public must “verify facts”

title: Jantar Mantar protest updates: Heavy security deployed at Jantar Mantar, as crowd of protesters swells
content: Rahul Gandhi using students' issues for political mileage, says J.P. Nadda; Wangchuk seeks 'unequivocal assurance' that no young protester will face punitive, retaliatory legal action

title: Jammu and Kashmir government identifies 654 vulnerable villages as wet weather to continue for next 24 hours
content: “As a precautionary measure, 1,505 people from vulnerable areas have already been shifted to designated rescue centres, while evacuation plans remain in place for other vulnerable locations,” says government spokesman

title: NHPC’s own 2024 paper recorded gas fires in Teesta-VI tunnels before fatal blast
content: The July 20 incident is likely to have occurred due to an isolated patch near the face of tunnel 4B, which was sudden and gave no indication or time for the safe evacuation of the people working inside the tunnel, the NHPC says

title: Amid Lok Sabha logjam, Centre offers debate on NEET paper leak, says street protests ‘won’t help’
content: Parliamentary Affairs Minister says government is prepared for a “detailed discussion”; Congress seeks adjournment motion to set aside all other business for the debate; demands Education Minister’s resignation

title: Delhi HC dismisses PIL over Sonam Wangchuk’s removal from Jantar Mantar
content: “Since an FIR is sought to be lodged in relation to a reportedly solitary incident, it is always open for the petitioner to take recourse to 173 of BNSS,” says court

title: NEET’s promise has failed; it’s time to trust States, writes M.K. Stalin
content: The student protests must serve as a wake-up call — not merely to improve exam security but to fundamentally rethink the entire framework of high-stakes national entrance exams

title: Maharashtra begins rollout of Farmer Loan Waiver Scheme; first 500 beneficiaries announced
content: Lists will be published at the village level in the Gram Panchayat, various executive cooperative society offices and the respective bank branches, says CMO

title: ‘Over 5,200 applications’: Ram Temple trust defers CEO selection by a month
content: In second meeting amid donation theft row, trust announces reconstituted committee of seers to oversee religious affairs of temple; secretary to be appointed on interim basis until CEO is picked

title: BJP, Oppn. take divergent view of police action on protesters
content: Monday’s march was ‘not peaceful’ and its organisers must be held accountable, says Delhi Minister; BJP MP says Centre acted with ‘sensitivity’; AAP highlights instances of women being ‘assaulted’; cadre standing with Rahul: Delhi Cong. chief

title: Diesel generator at textile showroom in Katpadi catches fire
content: The police said that it was around 2.30 p.m. when a few workers in the showroom noticed thick smoke coming from the diesel generator

title: Two-day Thiruvasagam conference to be held in Tiruvannamalai
content: As part of the event, around one lakh participants worldwide will participate, mainly virtually, in writing 10 compositions from Thiruvasagam

title: Meet us here, CJP tells govt. as crowd swells at Jantar Mantar
content: If Ministers want to talk, they should come to people, says CJP; group appeals to Wangchuk to end fast; people turn up in thousands at Jantar Mantar; some of the protesters injured in police action back at venue

title: Doctors in Kerala’s Nilambur save tribal woman, baby after rare uterine rupture
content: The 25-year-old woman from the Cheerakkuzhi tribal hamlet near Chugathara was brought to the Government District Hospital Nilambur hospital with a rare uterine rupture that left the baby lying inside her abdominal cavity

title: City hospital launches integrated robotic surgery programme
content: The initiative brings robotic-assisted surgery across multiple specialties under a common clinical framework

title: Doctor in Kerala’s Kannur booked over alleged medical negligence, hospital denies charges
content: The complainant alleges she was discharged after an endoscopic procedure despite a fish bone remaining lodged in her throat

title: Neeraj Chopra, Mirabai Chanu lead India's medal hopes at CWG 2026
content: India begins its Commonwealth Games campaign in Glasgow with a compact contingent.  The program features ten sports, a significant reduction from previous games.  Athletics forms the backbone of the campaign with many competitors.  Mirabai Chanu and Lovlina Borgohain are key figures for India.  Glasgow offers a learning opportunity before India hosts the games in 2030.

title: Tear gas, stones, bottles: Video shows late-night clash during CJP protest in Delhi
content: Fresh violence erupted during a Cockroach Janta Party protest in Delhi, injuring an assistant commissioner of police. Protesters allegedly hurled stones and bottles at security personnel deployed near Tolstoy Marg. CCTV footage from a July 20 protest corroborated police claims of protesters attacking security personnel. The Cockroach Janta Party rejected the police version, accusing them of excessive force against demonstrators.

title: Canada college to protesting Indian students: Seek legal advice, retain imp documents
content: Indian students protest after Portage College's business management diploma graduates faced work visa rejections.  The Immigration, Refugees and Citizenship Canada stated their programs were not eligible for permits.  Portage College encourages affected students to retain important documents and seek legal advice.  The college cannot overturn decisions made by the immigration authority.  Affected students should consult official government materials and obtain independent advice.

title: Google makes history with quarterly profit more than 459 of Fortune 500 firms’ revenue
content: Alphabet achieved a historic quarterly profit exceeding one hundred billion dollars.  This record profit was significantly boosted by investment gains from Anthropic and SpaceX.  Google Cloud revenue also saw a substantial increase, contributing to overall growth.  Despite these strong results, Alphabet's stock experienced a slight decline in trading.  Investors remain watchful of rising expenses and competition in the AI sector.

title: Office cab driver rapes Hyderabad woman on way to airport
content: A cabin crew member was allegedly raped by her cab driver while traveling to the airport. Police registered a rape case and arrested the 23-year-old driver from Shamshabad. The accused confessed to the offense during interrogation by police officials. He had taken a detour and forced himself upon the woman inside the cab. The woman contacted police with help and was sent for medical examination.

title: 2,000 people help reveal skeleton of giant predator older than dinosaurs
content: In an exciting collaboration, over 2,000 public volunteers are meticulously cleaning a colossal 1,300-kilogram fossilized skeleton of a Mastodonsaurus, an ancient amphibian that roamed the Earth 240 million years ago. Armed with specialized tools and ultraviolet light, these dedicated individuals are uncovering the mysteries of our planet's history. The spectacular skeleton is expected to be on display by October or November, showcasing humanity's connection to a distant past.

title: Prince George turns 13 as William and Kate release new birthday photo
content: Prince George has officially entered his teenage years. Prince William and Kate Middleton marked their eldest son's 13th birthday by sharing a new portrait and a heartwarming video on social media, celebrating the milestone just weeks before he begins a new chapter at Eton College. In September, he will begin secondary school at the prestigious Eton College in Windsor, following in the footsteps of his father, Prince William.

title: Ford & Aramark investigating Ford factory kiosks after employees lost jobs for 'stealing'
content: Ford and Aramark are reviewing workplace self-service kiosks. This review follows employee dismissals for alleged snack theft incidents. Workers claim payment failures and software glitches caused wrongful accusations. Some recently fired employees have been reinstated after investigations cleared them. The companies are investigating kiosk functionality issues in limited cases.

title: DDA launches housing scheme with 25% discount: 1,200+ flats, starting from Rs 33 lakh
content: The Delhi Development Authority has launched a new housing scheme for government employees and professionals. This scheme offers over twelve hundred ready-to-move flats with a significant twenty-five percent discount. Registration for these affordable homes opens on July twenty-fourth and booking begins August fifteenth. Flats are located in Narela and will be allotted on a first come, first served basis.

title: 'Youth welfare top priority': PM Modi announces fast-track courts for paper leak cases
content: Prime Minister Narendra Modi announced fast-track courts for paper leak cases. This move aims to ensure swift and stringent punishment for offenders. Opposition leader Rahul Gandhi criticized the Prime Minister, holding him responsible. Rahul reiterated student demands including the education minister's dismissal. The government vows strict action against those harming students' futures.

title: 'P*** Espanya': Spain's Ferran Torres WC mural vandalised in Barcelona
content: A mural honouring Spain's World Cup hero Ferran Torres was vandalised within 24 hours of being unveiled in Barcelona. Created by street artist TVBoy, it was repeatedly defaced with political graffiti, including "volem seleccions catalanes" ("we want Catalan national teams"), before being removed by city authorities. Despite its brief existence, the artwork highlighted the intersection of football, art and Catalan politics.

title: ‘Dhurandhar’ actress detained during Mumbai student protest; shares video
content: 'Dhurandhar' actress Ayesha Khan revealed she was detained by Mumbai Police on July 22 for standing on the road near the Jantar Mantar-inspired CJP protest. She shared videos from inside the police van, questioning the lack of explanation. Ayesha had earlier urged paparazzi and media to cover the Delhi protest.

title: R&B engineer who took up US job 20 years ago still on govt rolls
content: A Telangana engineer remains on government rolls after two decades abroad. He left for the US in 2006 without mandatory approval and returned briefly. The state government has initiated disciplinary proceedings against the engineer for misconduct. These proceedings aim to determine charges and decide further action for termination. The engineer has not submitted a defense statement to the inquiry authority.

title: Commonwealth Games 2026: India's full schedule, dates and events
content: A contingent of 125 Indian athletes will compete in Glasgow for the 2026 Commonwealth Games. India will participate across thirteen sports disciplines, including several para-sports events. Olympic champion Neeraj Chopra and Mirabai Chanu are expected to lead the medal charge for India. The schedule details daily events for Indian athletes from July 24 to August 2. Indian athletes aim to add to the nation's impressive Commonwealth Games medal history.

title: What happened to Charleston White? YouTuber shot at in Houston
content: Charleston White says he was shot at during a visit to Houston's Fifth Ward after a group allegedly confronted him over past remarks and asked him to apologize. In a livestream, the comedian confirmed he was not injured, shared his version of the incident, and said he plans to file a report with the FBI.

title: LeBron James could reunite with Kyrie Irving in Mavericks trade rumor
content: Kyrie Irving's name is circulating in Cleveland trade rumors again, nearly a decade after he requested the deal that broke up his title-winning partnership with LeBron James. A new insider report suggests Irving wouldn't shut the door on a return, with Dallas reportedly open to moving on as its timeline shifts toward Cooper Flagg.

title: More pulp, less tax: Govt hits sweet spot with 'aam' proposal to help Totapuri farmers
content: The government proposes a 5% GST rate for mango drinks with 22-25% real pulp. This move aims to revive Totapuri mango pulp demand and improve beverage quality. An expert committee studied Totapuri price declines and suggested solutions for farmers. Recommendations include price stabilization committees and orchard rejuvenation techniques. The government will consult ministries to finalize standards and the tax structure.

title: Cops in plain clothes wielding lathis during CJP protest: Necessity or ambiguity?
content: Plainclothes police involvement in protests drew criticism for lacking uniforms and identification. Senior officers explained this is necessary for intelligence gathering and undercover operations. Such deployments are not new and aim to manage large crowds effectively. While not strictly prohibited, it creates ambiguity during public demonstrations. Police leadership now discourages this practice unless situations escalate or manpower is short.

title: 'Hacker hai bhai hacker': CJP shares video of detained protesters fleeing Mumbai police van
content: A video showing Cockroach Janta Party protesters escaping a police van has gone viral. This incident occurred during ongoing tensions following a recent 'Sansad Chalo' march in Delhi. Protesters clashed with police near Parliament, leading to detentions and an FIR. The CJP is demanding accountability for alleged NEET examination irregularities. Activist Sonam Wangchuk continues his fast while undergoing hospital treatment.

title: Wife can't claim maintenance solely because she chose to live abroad: Bombay HC
content: The Bombay high court rejected a wife's plea for interim maintenance. She sought one lakh rupees monthly from her estranged husband. The court noted her substantial professional income and residence abroad. Simply citing high living costs abroad was deemed insufficient grounds. The ruling emphasized maintenance is for financially weaker parties needing support.

title: You think it's just another viral fever, but could it be your heart?
content: Seasonal illnesses often mimic heart attack symptoms, causing confusion for patients.  Doctors note that fatigue and chest discomfort can be mistaken for viral infections.  Subtle heart attack signs like nausea or jaw pain are easily overlooked.  Patients delay seeking help, believing symptoms are part of a common cold.  Prompt medical evaluation is crucial to prevent permanent heart damage.

title: Three Indian engineers left top careers to build India's quantum computer
content: Three former semiconductor engineers—Sujoy Chakravarty, Ravi Mehta and Biman Chattopadhyay—built a successful chip IP company, sold it to Synopsys, and launched Bengaluru-based Quanfluence to develop room-temperature quantum computers. After months of studying quantum technologies, they chose photonics as the most practical approach. Supported by academic experts, the startup now offers commercial cloud-based quantum computing solutions, demonstrating India's growing capabilities in frontier technology while highlighting the importance of lifelong learning, engineering excellence and innovation.

title: FBI nabs Harmanveer Singh over Canada-based gang's drug trafficking racket
content: An Indian national, Harmanveer Singh, was arrested in California for alleged drug trafficking.  He is linked to a Canada-based organized crime group led by Ravinder Dhanda.  This arrest is part of a larger international law enforcement operation targeting crime syndicates.  Authorities charged thirty-seven individuals under "Operation Hard Ball" across multiple countries.  Another Indian national, Nitish Kaushal, was also arrested while attempting to cross into Canada.

title: Rupee rises to 96.49 against US dollar, rising oil prices limit gains
content: The Indian rupee saw a slight gain against the US dollar on Thursday.  Rising crude oil prices continue to exert pressure on the currency.  The Reserve Bank of India's intervention is expected to limit sharper losses.  Foreign investors showed mixed sentiment in Indian equities and bonds.  Stock markets opened on a weak note, with key indices falling.

title: Aamir Khan's nephew Imran joins protest: 'I'm here to show support and solidarity'
content: Actor Imran Khan joined a student protest in Mumbai on Wednesday. He expressed solidarity with the young demonstrators participating in a peaceful gathering. Videos of Imran Khan marching amid a downpour are now going viral online. He condemned the reported use of force against student protesters in his statement. Imran Khan had previously posted his strong condemnation of police action.

title: 'Put your own house in order': India slams Pakistan over crossborder terror at UN
content: India strongly criticized Pakistan's continued use of crossborder terrorism as a state policy.  Ambassador Parvathaneni Harish stated Jammu and Kashmir is an inalienable part of India.  He highlighted Pakistan's illegal occupation as the sole unresolved issue concerning the region.  Crossborder terrorism has eroded the trust needed for bilateral cooperation, India noted.  Pakistan should focus on its domestic challenges instead of targeting India.

title: Ram temple trust puts off appointment of CEO and 3 members
content: The Shri Ram Janmabhoomi Teerth Kshetra Trust deferred key appointments and restructured religious oversight.  A nine-member committee, including five seers, will now manage temple responsibilities.  Decisions on the chief executive officer and new trustees were postponed for further review.  The trust expressed dissatisfaction with SBI's donation counting system and replaced staff.  Public faith was not perceived as dented by donation counting controversies.

title: Did Russia aid Iranian drone attacks on CIA sites? What report says
content: US intelligence agencies are investigating Russia's potential role in recent Iranian drone attacks.  Analysts are examining the precision and effectiveness of these strikes.  Russia may have provided targeting intelligence and advanced drone technology.  This involvement could mark a significant escalation in undermining US operations.  At least two CIA facilities were targeted in March.

title: Vijay fans burst firecrackers inside packed theatre during 'Jana Nayagan' screening
content: Fans celebrated Vijay's film 'Jana Nayagan' by bursting firecrackers inside a Bengaluru theatre. This unusual celebration drew widespread attention and mixed reactions online from viewers. The incident occurred during the screening of the actor's final film before entering politics. Many moviegoers recorded the moment, which quickly went viral on social media platforms. The event highlighted fan passion and reignited safety discussions within cinema halls.

title: Candace Owens slams Erika Kirk, says Charlie Kirk fueled her fame ambitions
content: Candace Owens intensified her public feud with Erika Kirk, accusing her of pursuing fame and fortune throughout her life. In a podcast, Owens claimed Charlie Kirk was Erika's "ticket to more fame" and argued a recent article criticizing her instead exposed Erika's character. She also questioned Erika's motives, saying her focus had always been on public attention, money, and influence.

title: Blake Lively's new inner circle fuels Taylor Swift friendship buzz
content: Blake Lively has sparked fresh speculation about her friendship with Taylor Swift after sharing photos with a new group of friends following her absence from Swift and Travis Kelce's wedding. While fans continue to question their relationship, Lively remains focused on new friendships as her legal dispute with Justin Baldoni continues.

title: Caleb Williams vs Patrick Mahomes: Who had the stronger NFL rookie season?
content: Caleb Williams impressed with a productive rookie season, showing poise and playmaking ability despite difficult circumstances. However, Patrick Mahomes made the greater early impact overall. After spending his rookie year learning, Mahomes exploded in his first season as a starter, winning NFL MVP, throwing for 5,000 yards and 50 touchdowns, and setting a benchmark few young quarterbacks have matched.

title: 'I'm sorry': Why Argentina's assistant coach apologised after FIFA World Cup final
content: Argentina assistant coach Roberto Ayala apologized for his actions during a post-match altercation.  He admitted his behavior was unacceptable after Spain defeated Argentina in the World Cup final.  Ayala expressed regret for losing control and stated his intention was to separate players.  He accepted full responsibility for his actions during the chaotic post-game scenes.  FIFA has launched an investigation into the post-match brawl involving players and staff.

title: 'When you fear failure, you play to save yourself': Shreyas Iyer's message to Team India
content: India captain Shreyas Iyer wants players to avoid fear of failure.  He stressed focusing on expressing themselves freely on the field.  This comes after a disappointing white-ball tour of England and Ireland.  The team aims to adapt quickly to new conditions in Zimbabwe.  Iyer believes this challenging phase will make the team stronger.

title: 'Will Ask Shehbaz Sharif To Make Me PM': Nawaz Sharif's New PoK 'Desire'
content: Sharif claimed PoK was as important for him as his home province, Punjab. "It is my second home. My ancestors migrated from Kashmir."

title: 'PPE Funds To Buy Rolexes, Cars': Indian-Origin Couple's Covid Fraud In UK
content: The NCA's investigation found bank records showing Bhandari and his wife used the money to buy Rolexes, jewellery, holiday packages, and cars.

title: Parliament Monsoon Session 2026 Live Updates: NDA MPs To Protest Against Congress At Parliament's Makar Dwar
content: Parliament Monsoon Session 2026 Live Updates: A day earlier, the BJP alleged that Congress MP and Lok Sabha Leader of Opposition (LoP), Rahul Gandhi, was running away from the duties of the Opposition and instead "only attempting to create a ruckus".

title: Delhi Cop Who Slapped Woman During CJP Protest Removed From Jantar Mantar
content: Sandeep Lamba, an Additional Deputy Commissioner of Police, was seen slapping a female CJP protester on July 20.

title: Gold, Flats, Vehicles: Telangana Engineer Found With Rs 150 Crore Assets
content: Officials detected bank deposits of Rs 52.02 lakh, 2.094 kg of gold ornaments worth about Rs 4 crore, three vehicles (Kia, Mahindra and Maruti Baleno) worth around Rs 45 lakh

title: FBI Arrests Indian National Harmanveer Singh In Drug Trafficking Case
content: Officers with the California Highway Patrol arrested Harmanveer Singh in Stockton on Tuesday, weeks after a federal warrant was issued in June.

title: Stock Market LIVE Updates, Sensex Today: Sensex Drops 180 Points As Oil Touches $95 Amid US-Iran War
content: Stock Market, Sensex, Share Market, Nifty LIVE Updates: Crude oil prices climb to six-week high with Brent above $95 amid escalating US-Iran war.

title: Chhattisgarh Chief Minister Says Uniform Code Bill Likely In Winter Session
content: The chief minister stated that, in addition to the "Mahatari Vandan Yojana," the state government is taking several major steps to empower women economically.

title: Analysis: Why Congress's Charanjit Channi Dilemma Could Be BJP's Gain
content: Channi also attempted another move to oppose the decision; however, the high command is firm on continuing with Amrinder Singh Raja Warring.

title: Tesla Profit Falls As Musk Ramps Up Spending On AI, Robotaxis
content: Tesla's second-quarter profit dropped 12% as R&D spending jumped 49%, even as revenue and vehicle sales beat Wall Street expectations

title: CJP Protest LIVE Updates: 'Won't Spare Those Who Harm Future Of Our Youth,' Says PM Modi On Paper Leaks
content: CJP Jantar Mantar protest LIVE: The Cockroach Janta Party (CJP) protesters are continuing their sit-in at Jantar Mantar.

title: Gold Gets Costlier, Silver Shines As Budget Buyers Rethink Jewellery
content: Rising gold prices are certainly encouraging more consumers to look at silver, but the shift goes much deeper than affordability alone.

title: "Want Better Future": 13-Year-Old On Why She Joined CJP's Protest
content: Many young men and women said the protesters are at the protest site for all students and children, who are the future of the nation.

title: Siya Goyal, Lover Wanted To Injure Fiance, But Later Dropped 'Risky' Plan
content: Siya Goyal, 20, and her 22-year-old lover, Chetan Chaudhary, allegedly pushed her 26-year-old fiance, Ketan Agarwal, into a gorge from the Lohagad Fort near Pune on June 18.

title: End-Of-Season Sale Is Here. But Big Discounts Can Cost You More Money
content: Many brands have shortened sale periods, making genuine "80 per cent off everything" offers less common than shoppers may expect.

title: Iran-US War News Highlights: US Strikes Iran For 12th Consecutive Night
content: US-Iran War News Highlights: The US State Department has issued an alert urging American citizens around the world to "exercise increased caution" over the war with Iran. The alert said that "due to heightened tensions in the Middle East.

title: Iran-US War Live: Houthis Attack Saudi Tankers In Red Sea As US Hits Iran Again
content: US-Iran War News Live Updates: US President Donald Trump had threatened to destroy an Iranian bridge or power plant for every attack on shipping in Hormuz, drawing an "eye for an eye" warning from Tehran as both sides sharpened threats in the escalating war.

title: Congress, CJP Protest Highlights: "I Want To Live, But Not At Cost of Youth," Says Sonam Wangchuk
content: Congress, CJP Protest Highlights: The huge protest over the NEET paper leak are continuing for the third straight day today.

title: Uttarakhand Nurse, Seeking A Job, Consumes Phenyl Outside Minister's House
content: An unemployed female nurse, seeking a job in the health department, allegedly consumed phenyl outside the residence of state health minister Subodh Uniyal in Dehradun.

title: ‘My mother doesn’t know I am injured’: Protesters stand firm at Jantar Mantar
content: Koushiki Saha, a trainee journalist currently reporting for The Times of India, covers urban governance, infrastructure lapses, public grievances, and municipal policies with clarity and compassion.
